title: Track robotics training dynamics in Weights & Biases
transcript: [music] >> Hi, I'm Manish a product manager at Weights & Biases by CoreWeave. Today, I'm going to quickly walk you through how to train and analyze your robotic models within Weights & Biases. We have trained a a humanoid robot to walk using reinforcement learning. And we are using tools such as NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Isaac Sim which has first-class integration with Weights & Biases. So, you just have to put in your API key and all of your training metrics starts flowing in. What we are doing today is we are trying to find a good hyperparameter for learning rate. Learning rate is just enables the model to tune how quickly it can learn. And we have trained our model on H100 GPUs by CoreWeave on the CoreWeave platform specifically using CKS of CoreWeave Kubernetes which significantly helps speed up the entire workflow. It would have taken a few weeks and I was able to do it within one or two days. And then finally, for visualization we used tracking visualization. We used Weights & Biases as I mentioned. So, quickly jumping to the workspace. As you can see on the left-hand side you have a bunch of runs. And then on the right-hand side you have all the metrics and some evaluation videos and you have some some reinforcement learning plots. Now, one of the things that as a researcher I'm looking at is okay, I have a bunch of runs that I've selected. It's very hard for me to find the baseline. I have to go rename things and whatnot. So, recently we enabled a feature for you to quickly set a baseline. And once you've set the baseline and potentially this is the the best checkpoint that I have. So, I can also do well and pin a specific run. So, as you can see wherever I am in this run selector even if I have thousands of runs your baseline and your pinned runs are always anchored to the top. So, you can you have it's pretty easily accessible. On the right you can see there is there is parallel coordinate plots where you can see how your specific hyperparameter has impacted your expected outcome or your in this case my output is mean reward. So, I'm looking at which learning rate is significantly providing me or giving me a highest reward. It's just one of the metrics, right? I'm also looking for other [clears throat] data which is which is how how volatile the training is and some other data as well. So, now if you can see if I hover over you can easily compare your baseline with other runs. And the baseline can be easily seen by this red color ribbon or the color. Quickly, now if I want to look at all the runs having a common or very specific learning rate what I can do is we enable semantic run colors. So, you can go ahead and select colors and runs as key-based colors. In this case, the key is learning rate. And I would define specific categories. You can see that all all the learning rates that are same have now same colors. It's very easy for me to find >> [clears throat] >> the learning rates which are exactly same or similar and how they are behaving overall across my plots. As you can see the yellow learning rates they they are pretty similar or pretty same and they all >> [clears throat] >> are they all have the same color. So, it's easy for you to quickly identify how your metrics or training is behaving with respect to a specific training config. Okay, I'll go ahead and revert this back to original project colors. The third thing is that uh we now have the ability to visualize all of your videos side by side. So, there's a new side by side comparison. So, if you go to your media panel, click settings and and layout you can now compare you have a new mode compare mode. And then you can easily select a specific baseline or specific run. You can also set a specific key, go to a specific step or index. Then these are all are linked to all of these runs. You can also change it as per your needs. But you can now compare any media across your checkpoints side by side. So, in this case for my robotic workflow I want to see how all how is my robot performing across all of these checkpoints. And as you can see during my earlier checkpoints >> [clears throat] >> the robot has not yet start learned how to walk. So, they they're just falling over. But as I progress my training the robot has has learned how to walk. But there are two very aggressive learning rates which as which didn't did not work in evaluation. Uh And [clears throat] I have I see there's good checkpoint or the next best checkpoint compared with baseline. And I have this as a second column here. And now one of the things that we have also launched or enabled is synchronous video playback. So, you can go to media panel sync and say sync I want to synchronize video. And as you can see if I play all of the videos here play together in synchronous. I can scrub all of these videos together or pause all of these videos together. So, it really helps me quickly move or scrub videos and see compare side by side how a particular robot performed at specific point across my checkpoints. Okay. >> [clears throat] >> So, that's the evaluation videos. And I want to highlight one additional thing which is the system metrics. So, as part of as you uh train all of your models on CoreWeave platform, we also pull in all of your system metrics right on into your Weights & Biases workspace. You can see you have your GPU power usage, all of your any errors that that might have come up. And now you can easily correlate if there is a spike or if there is a problem with your your respective metric, you can easily correlate all of your metrics with all of your system metrics with that anomaly in your in your in your metrics in your training metrics. So, finally what I want to you to take away is that training all of your robotic models on CoreWeave with H100s and RTX Pro 6000 really helps you quickly train your model but also get photorealistic photorealistic environments as well. And >> [clears throat] >> and and with rich media features and experiment experiment at tracking in Weights & Biases you get significant speed in your experiments. And then also the visibility that you need to understand and analyze your experiments. Thank you. >> [music] [music]
